articles.title: Freya Ridings: A Singer's Brave Journey
articles.summary: Freya Ridings is a singer who became famous with her first album. She felt very nervous and had panic attacks when making her second album. She was forced to work with big producers in studios that made her uncomfortable. Her record label changed, and her manager left, making things hard. Freya felt scared and lost her confidence. She decided to buy a plane ticket to take control of her career. Now, she is making music her own way and feeling better. This story shows how artists can face challenges and find their voice again.

articles.title: Aryna Sabalenka Wins Big Tennis Tournament
articles.summary: Aryna Sabalenka is a top tennis player from Belarus. She won the Miami Open tournament by beating Coco Gauff in a close match. The score was 6-2, 4-6, 6-3. This win makes her the first woman since 2022 to win both the Indian Wells and Miami tournaments in the same year, called the 'Sunshine Double'. Sabalenka has won three tournaments this year and lost only one match. She is very proud of her hard work and fighting spirit. Coco Gauff played well too and will move up in the world rankings. Tennis is a sport where players hit a ball over a net, and tournaments like this are exciting events.

articles.title: Scientists Make Solar Cells Work Better Than Ever
articles.summary: Solar cells are devices that use sunlight to make electricity, helping us fight climate change by reducing our need for fossil fuels like coal and oil. Normally, solar cells can only use about one-third of the sunlight that hits them because some light has too little energy and some loses extra energy as heat. Scientists from Japan and Germany have found a new way to make solar cells work much better. They used a special material called a molybdenum-based metal complex to capture extra energy from a process called singlet fission. This process can create two energy particles from one, allowing the solar cells to achieve over 130% efficiency, which is higher than the usual 100% limit. This breakthrough could lead to more powerful solar panels in the future, making clean energy more effective and affordable.

articles.title: A New Material to Catch Carbon Dioxide Cheaply
articles.summary: Scientists have made a new material that can help catch carbon dioxide, a gas that causes climate change. Catching carbon dioxide is important to stop it from going into the air, but it is often too expensive. The new material is called viciazites, and it is made from carbon with special nitrogen parts placed next to each other in a neat way. This helps it trap carbon dioxide better and release it using less heat, which saves energy and money. The researchers tested different versions and found that some work very well, especially one that releases carbon dioxide at low temperatures. This could make carbon capture cheaper and easier to use in the future. The material might also help with other jobs like cleaning water.

articles.title: Jannik Sinner Wins Tennis Match to Reach Final
articles.summary: Jannik Sinner, a top tennis player from Italy, beat Alexander Zverev in a tennis match to reach the final of the Miami Open tournament. The match score was 6-3 7-6 (7-4). Sinner has won many matches in a row, including against Zverev before. He will play against Jiri Lehecka in the final on Sunday. Sinner is trying to win the Miami Open again and achieve a special double win after winning another tournament recently. He is happy to be in the final and said it was a tough match. Lehecka also won his match to reach his first big final and is excited but remembers there are more important things in the world.

articles.title: How the President and Oil Prices Dance Together
articles.summary: This article talks about how the US president, Donald Trump, and oil prices move together like a dance. Oil is a type of fuel that comes from the ground and is used for cars and factories. When the president says things about fighting with Iran, oil prices can go up or down. For example, before the fighting started, oil cost about $72 per barrel. Later, it went up to $118 and then down to $112. Investors, who are people that buy and sell things to make money, watch the president's words closely because they affect oil prices. Sometimes, the president's comments might be to change the price of oil, not just to talk about plans. This makes it tricky for investors to know what to do. The article uses charts to show these changes over the last month.
